output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 7/7.
Batch 1: Implemented batch 1 / T1 by adding a shared mobile interaction policy model, threading it through `useTimelineState` into the split editor data/ops contexts and `VideoEditorProvider`, and expanding provider coverage so consumers can read/update the policy through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without calling `useTimelineState()` directly. `interactionStateRef` remains the only save/poll deferral gate. Checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Implemented batch 2 / T2 by making the shell and inspector read/write the shared interaction policy: `VideoEditorShell` now shows a phone mode bar, uses selection-aware inspector targets, and surfaces a quicker tablet inspector affordance in condensed layouts; `PropertiesPanel` and `ClipPanel` now expose inspector-first actions for trim, move, track moves, split, mute, delete, and precision so touch users do not have to rely on direct manipulation. Checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Implemented batch 3 / T3 by wiring modality-aware gesture ownership through the existing timeline stack: `TimelineEditor` now passes the shared interaction policy into marquee, clip drag, ruler, and canvas flows; `TimeRuler` is the dedicated scrub owner; empty-space touch drag stays pan-first unless marquee is explicitly allowed; and clip trim now delays pointer capture, `preventDefault()`, and `interactionStateRef.current.resize` until a real resize gesture starts. Verified with type-checking and the full `TimelineCanvas` and `useClipDrag` test modules, then checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Implemented batch 4 / T4 by applying touch-aware selection and move rules, adding visible clip and shot-group action triggers for phone/tablet layouts, and wiring precision mode into keyboard seeking without changing desktop or hardware-keyboard defaults. Touch `Select` mode now toggles clip membership, touch `Move` mode preserves an additive selection unless a drag actually starts, and non-hover touch entry points now expose clip and shot-group actions that previously depended on context-menu or hover-only affordances. Verified with type-checking plus the full `ClipAction`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `useClipDrag` test modules, then checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Implemented batch 5 / T5 by moving preview overlay editing onto the shared interaction contract: `OverlayEditor` now uses pointer-driven preview ownership, phone touch stays inspector-first, preview/background taps route through shared context state, and shell plus preview controls now expose larger touch targets, live interaction-state labels, and reduced-motion-safe transitions. Verified with type-checking and the full `PreviewPersistence` and `RemotionPreview` test modules, then checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_5.json`.
Batch 6: Implemented batch 6 / T6 by extending the existing editor test surface in-place: added pure interaction decision-table assertions, expanded provider publication coverage, added touch trim gating and reachable shot-group touch-action coverage, added tablet touch-vs-mouse drag routing and touch move-mode drag arming coverage, extended clip overflow touch-entry coverage, and added deferred-save regression coverage proving saves stay blocked until both drag and resize are idle. Verified with type-checking and a single full Vitest run across the required modules, then checkpointed the batch state to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_6.json`.
Batch 7: Automated verification for T7 passed: `tsc` and the targeted editor Vitest modules were green, the temporary iPad pointer-arbitration repro passed, and the repro script was removed. T7 remains incomplete only because the required manual Safari smoke checks could not be performed from this terminal-only environment.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `mobile-interaction-model` and threaded `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `precisionEnabled`, `contextTarget`, and `inspectorTarget` through `useTimelineState()` into the split editor data/ops contexts and `VideoEditorProvider`. Provider coverage now proves consumers can read and update the policy through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without bypassing the provider. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`. `interactionStateRef` remains the only save/poll gate because `useTimelineSave()` still receives only that ref and no second persistence suppression path was added.

Made shell and inspector affordances policy-driven without introducing a second mobile editor path. `VideoEditorShell` now reads the shared interaction policy, renders a persistent phone mode bar for `Browse`/`Select`/`Move`/`Trim` plus a `Precision` toggle, keeps the inspector target in sync with selection, and surfaces a selection-aware inspector affordance in condensed tablet layouts that can jump directly to properties. `PropertiesPanel` and `ClipPanel` now provide explicit inspector-first controls for trim, move, move-to-track via track up/down, split, mute, delete, and precision so common touch edits do not depend on fragile direct manipulation. Verified with `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` and `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.

Implemented modality-aware timeline arbitration without introducing a second mobile timeline or a second persistence gate. `TimelineEditor` now passes `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `setInputModalityFromPointerType`, and `setGestureOwner` into `useClipDrag`, `useMarqueeSelect`, `TimeRuler`, and `TimelineCanvas`. `TimeRuler` now claims the dedicated scrub zone through shared gesture ownership. `useMarqueeSelect` keeps empty-space touch drag pan-first unless tablet touch selection mode explicitly allows marquee. `useClipDrag` continues to set `interactionStateRef.current.drag` only after drag threshold. `TimelineCanvas` now expands touch trim handles in trim mode and defers resize pointer capture, `preventDefault()`, `pendingOpsRef`, and `interactionStateRef.current.resize` until the resize gesture crosses threshold, preserving `interactionStateRef` as the only save/poll deferral gate. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`.

Applied the selection, move, context, shot-group, and precision contract through the existing provider-backed editor stack. Added touch-selection helpers to the shared interaction model, taught `useClipDrag` to treat touch `Select` taps as toggle selection and touch `Move` taps as selection-preserving when a drag never starts, and extended `useMultiSelect` / `useTimelineSelection` with a no-collapse preserve path so additive selections survive touch move attempts. Added visible overflow affordances for clip and shot-group actions on phone/tablet layouts so navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching are reachable without hover or `onContextMenu`-only entry points. Added precision-aware keyboard seeking with frame-step `Alt` + arrow behavior gated by precision mode, while leaving desktop and hardware-keyboard defaults unchanged otherwise. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`.

Brought preview overlay editing onto the same provider-backed interaction contract without adding a second mobile preview path. `PreviewPanel` now resolves preview background interactions through pointer events, publishes preview interaction state for assistive tech, and passes `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, and gesture-owner setters into `OverlayEditor`. `OverlayEditor` now uses pointer events plus preview gesture ownership, claims `preview` only for actual overlay transforms, keeps phone touch inspector-first by skipping direct transform starts and hiding touch-direct manipulation controls there, and enlarges touch-visible preview controls to 44x44 targets where direct manipulation remains allowed. `VideoEditorShell` now applies larger touch targets to shell and preview action controls, adds live screen-reader labels for interaction state, and adds reduced-motion-safe transition fallbacks. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.test.tsx`.

Extended the existing editor coverage without adding a second save path or new standalone suites. Added pure interaction decision-table assertions to `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, expanded `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` to verify touch trim-handle gating and reachable touch shot-group actions, expanded `useClipDrag.test.tsx` to cover tablet touch-vs-mouse drag routing and touch move-mode drag arming, expanded `ClipAction.test.tsx` to cover labeled visible overflow entry points for multi-selection, expanded `PreviewPersistence.test.tsx` to lock the phone-mode touch target regression, and expanded `useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx` to verify saves remain deferred until both drag and resize are idle. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx`.

Ran the final automated verification loop successfully with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.test.tsx`; all 7 modules passed. Wrote a temporary repro script for tablet `deviceClass` plus `pointerType` arbitration, ran it successfully with `node --import tsx .megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/tmp-pointer-arbitration-repro.ts`, and removed the script afterward. sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The interaction policy is now published on `useTimelineEditorData()` and updated through `useTimelineEditorOps()` via `VideoEditorProvider`, so shell, timeline, preview, and properties consumers do not need to call `useTimelineState()` directly. `interactionStateRef` is still the sole save/poll gate because the persistence path still only passes that ref into `useTimelineSave()` and the new policy state does not participate in save suppression.

Yes. Phone layouts now expose a persistent mode bar and inspector button that both route through the shared interaction policy, while touch-oriented inspector action groups in `PropertiesPanel` and `ClipPanel` make trim, move, track moves, split, mute, delete, and precision visible without relying on direct timeline manipulation. Condensed tablet layouts also surface a selection-aware `Clip`/`Selection` inspector affordance and auto-open the properties side when a selection is present, so access is faster than relying on the preview/properties toggle alone.

Yes. Ruler scrub, empty-canvas marquee, clip drag, and trim now resolve ownership through the shared interaction policy using both baseline device class and per-event `pointerType`. `TimeRuler` owns scrub gestures as the dedicated ruler zone, touch empty-space interactions stay pan-first unless tablet touch select mode explicitly allows marquee, touch clip drag only arms in move mode, and trim claims `trim` ownership only after the resize threshold is crossed. Because touch-specific gating is resolved from `pointerType`, iPad mouse and trackpad still follow desktop behavior instead of inheriting iPad touch rules.

Yes. Phone and iPad touch selection and move behavior now follow the shared interaction contract instead of desktop-only modifier assumptions: touch `Select` mode toggles clip membership, touch `Move` mode preserves additive selections unless a drag actually begins, and desktop plus hardware-keyboard semantics remain on the existing path because the new branching only activates for touch modality. Clip and shot-group actions that were previously hover- or context-menu-gated now have visible touch triggers, and precision mode adds frame-step arrow-key seeking without replacing the existing desktop shortcut behavior.

Yes. Preview overlay editing now follows the same interaction contract as the timeline: overlay transforms start from pointer events, claim shared `gestureOwner` as `preview`, and release that ownership when the interaction ends. Phone touch remains inspector-first because overlay transform handles and preview-side transform buttons are hidden there and touch pointer-down only selects and routes focus back to inspector state, while tablet touch and mouse/trackpad paths keep direct manipulation. `PreviewPanel` and `VideoEditorShell` also add live interaction-state labels, larger touch targets, and reduced-motion-safe transitions so preview controls stay accessible without stealing touches from the mode bar, inspector, or timeline.

Yes. The updated automated coverage stays within the existing test infrastructure and now exercises the shared interaction contract across provider publication, pure routing decisions, touch trim gating, tablet touch-vs-mouse drag behavior, visible touch shot-group and clip action entry points, and deferred-save behavior. The persistence coverage still runs through the existing `interactionStateRef` gate and explicitly proves there is no second save path by keeping save deferred until both drag and resize are idle.

No. The targeted suites passed after reruns, the temporary pointer-arbitration repro passed, and the repro script was removed from the repo, but the required manual Safari smoke checks on iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad could not be performed from this terminal-only environment.
